{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"Taconite is a geological substance defined by its mineralogical composition (iron minerals interlayered with quartz and chert), its specific iron content (20–30%), and its origin in Precambrian banded iron formations of the Mesabi Range. These defining properties require technical geology, mineralogy, and stratigraphy beyond everyday language and the kernel catalog. It is a technical earth-science concept, not expressible in the allowed vocabulary."}
